requirement: Fix e2e test failures, add observability (metrics + tracing), clean up dead code, improve health checks

tasks.title: Fix e2e test failures — response format mismatches
tasks.description:
Fix the 6 documented e2e test failures:

1. In tests/e2e/e2e_test.go or tests/e2e/lifecycle_test.go — fix tests that expect JSON objects but receive arrays from list endpoints. The clearing/netting/margin/settlement endpoints CORRECTLY return arrays. Update the e2e tests to handle array responses (use []interface{} or check first element of array).

2. Fix compliance KYC test — check if the compliance-service KYC endpoint returns array or object. If it's a creation endpoint (POST), it should return the created object. Check src/compliance-service/ handler to verify and fix either the handler or the test.

3. Gateway 404 routing — check if the RouteChecker middleware (added in earlier sprint) is properly returning 404 for unknown paths BEFORE auth runs. Read src/gateway/internal/middleware/auth.go and src/gateway/cmd/gateway/main.go to verify middleware ordering. If auth still runs before routing on unknown paths, fix the middleware chain.

Run the Go unit tests for gateway and compliance-service to verify fixes don't break anything: cd src/gateway && go test ./... && cd ../compliance-service && go test ./...

tasks.title: Add OpenTelemetry trace context propagation
tasks.description:
Add W3C traceparent header propagation to all services.

In src/shared/internal/observability/tracing.go (new file):
1. Parse W3C traceparent header: version-trace_id-parent_id-trace_flags (e.g., 00-4bf92f3577b6a27520e8ecf678db76-00f067aa0ba902b7-01)
2. Generate trace_id (16 bytes hex) and span_id (8 bytes hex) if no incoming traceparent
3. Create TraceContext struct: TraceID, SpanID, ParentSpanID, Sampled
4. TraceMiddleware HTTP middleware that: extracts traceparent from request, creates span context, injects traceparent into response header, adds trace_id to request context
5. Helper to extract TraceContext from context.Context
6. Helper to inject traceparent into outgoing HTTP requests (for service-to-service calls)

In src/shared/internal/observability/tracing_test.go:
- Test traceparent parsing (valid, invalid, missing)
- Test trace_id generation
- Test middleware propagation
- Test context injection/extraction

Keep zero-dependency: implement W3C traceparent format manually (it's just hex string parsing). Do NOT import opentelemetry-go.

Wire TraceMiddleware into gateway middleware chain in src/gateway/cmd/gateway/main.go (after RequestID, before auth).

Add trace_id to the existing logger output in src/shared/internal/observability/logger.go if there's a structured logger.

tasks.title: Add /metrics endpoint and wire to all services
tasks.description:
Enhance the existing metrics in src/shared/internal/observability/ and wire them into all 9 Go services.

The metrics.go already has request_count and request_duration_seconds. Enhance it:
1. Add http_request_errors_total counter (5xx responses)
2. Add http_response_size_bytes histogram
3. Add a /metrics HTTP handler that serves Prometheus text format (already partially implemented — verify and fix)
4. Add MetricsServer that runs on a separate port (service_port + 100, e.g., matching-engine 8081 → metrics on 8181)

Wire into each service's main.go or server.go:
- matching-engine (8081 → metrics 8181)
- clearing-engine (8082 → metrics 8182)
- margin-engine (8083 → metrics 8183)
- settlement-engine (8084 → metrics 8184)
- auth-service → metrics on next available
- compliance-service → metrics on next available
- gateway (8080 → metrics 8180)
- market-data-service → metrics on next available
- warehouse-service → metrics on next available

For each service, add MetricsMiddleware to the HTTP handler chain.

Add tests: src/shared/internal/observability/metrics_test.go — test counter increments, histogram recording, /metrics text format output.

Keep zero-dependency: the existing Prometheus text format implementation is manual — enhance it, don't replace with prometheus/client_golang.

tasks.title: Clean up dead code + health check improvements
tasks.description:
1. Remove src/clearing-service/ directory entirely (it's a stub with only a Dockerfile — the real implementation is in src/clearing-engine/).

2. Add readiness probes to all services. The gateway already has /healthz (liveness) and /readyz (readiness). Add the same pattern to other services:
- Each service should have /healthz (always 200 if process is running — liveness)
- Each service should have /readyz (200 only when the service has completed initialization and can serve requests — readiness)
- Use the existing pattern from src/gateway/internal/handler/health.go (atomic readyFlag)

3. Add a /healthz/dependencies endpoint to the gateway that checks connectivity to all backend services and returns aggregate health status. This gives operators a single endpoint to verify the full platform health.

4. Update docker-compose.yml healthcheck directives to use /healthz for each service.

Run: go test ./... in each modified service to verify.

tasks.title: Integration test — verify all fixes
tasks.description:
Run the full test suite across all Go services to verify T1-T4 changes don't break anything:
1. Run Go tests for all 9 services: for each service in src/*/; do cd $service && go test ./... && cd ../..; done
2. Run the e2e tests (they'll skip if gateway isn't running, that's fine — verify they compile)
3. Verify src/clearing-service/ no longer exists
4. Verify /metrics, /healthz, /readyz endpoints exist in service code
5. Run admin-ui tests: cd src/admin-ui && npm test -- --run
6. Report pass/fail counts per service
